extraction.one_line_summary: POGACAR won atop Jebel Hafeet after ALMEIDA's domestique acceleration forced YATES to chase, allowing Pogacar to sit on and outsprint him at the line.
tactical_patterns: UAE used ALMEIDA's GC position as tactical weapon, forcing YATES to chase while POGACAR sat on with <2km remaining.
BILBAO dragged ALMEIDA up to YATES/POGACAR group, inadvertently giving UAE's leader a free tow through mid-climb.
CONRAD's extended pull for VLASOV caught final breakaway rider with 9km left, ideal tempo-setting zone for GC teams.

rider_signals.YATES Adam: negative: forced to do all pacing work in final 2km while Pogacar sat on, tactically isolated without teammates
rider_signals.ALMEIDA Joao: positive: strong enough to attack and distance Bilbao with <2km left despite being in young rider jersey support role
rider_signals.BILBAO Pello: neutral: faded final 2km after unwittingly towing Almeida to leaders, suggesting strong but not elite climbing form
rider_signals.GUERREIRO Ruben: negative: suffered mechanical issues on climb approach, catastrophic timing for EF's GC ambitions
rider_signals.PLAPP Lucas: positive: fifth place finish shows Australian climbing beyond expected capabilities on 11% gradients at 31C
